attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-ENT-01-00201 — ingest --reseq to assign the real sequence within the SPEC-ENT-01 bucket.

Specialty tag: the 00_case_envelope specialty_tags enum has no 'ENT' value, so specialty_tags is set to ['ID'] (this is an ear infection; source category is Common Infections & Afflictions) while the case_id prefix retains 'ENT' (otolaryngology). Per the taxonomy id_rule the id is an opaque name and case_metadata is authoritative — confirm on review.
Age not stated in source ('solitary adult', Wt 78 kg only) — age set provisionally to 40 to satisfy the required demographics.age field; confirm on clinician review.
Sex at birth not stated in source — set provisionally to 'male' to satisfy the required demographics.sex_at_birth enum (no 'unknown' value permitted); confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit with otoscopy, pinna-traction/tragal palpation, preauricular node palpation and clinician vitals. Otoscopic canal findings, palpated lymphadenopathy, granulation-absence and vitals are sealed in 10/11; patient-observable temperature, video-visible canal-opening erythema/discharge, self-elicited tragal pain and muffled hearing were reprojected to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false).

Tier calibration: baseline set to T2 (prompt GP/telehealth prescriber), NOT the source note's 'urgent after-hours' framing which is an in-person MBS billing rationale rather than true acuity. Necrotising otitis externa is retained only as a conservative safety-net (patient is non-diabetic, immunocompetent, no systemic toxicity).

Candidate codes only (SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT), all unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; differential SNOMED codes in node 10 are best-effort candidates. No dose was synthesised beyond the source note's ciprofloxacin/hydrocortisone regimen and standard OTC analgesia dosing; adult patient, no paediatric dosing involved.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
